channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: US to Explore Allowing Driverless Cars Without Steering Wheels
item.description: auto safety regulator said the agency “absolutely” will consider ending requirements that driverless cars include steering wheels, a potential boon for Tesla Inc. The changes, part of an ongoing effort by the Trump administration to modernize rules for driverless cars, could ease the path for purpose-built autonomous vehicles including Tesla’s Cybercab, a two-seat electric car that lacks a steering wheel or foot pedals. Tesla Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk has advocated for policy changes that would support broad commercial deployment of self-driving vehicles, including calling for a federal framework for driverless cars.

item.title: New Data Shows Risk of Drowning Highest for Kids Ages 1-4
item.description: Drowning is the leading cause of death for children ages one to four, according to a U.S. CPSC’s latest data shows that for children in the U.S. “Drowning remains the leading cause of death for children ages one through four, and this year’s report makes clear that these tragedies continue to take an unacceptable toll on American families,” said Acting CPSC Chairman Peter A.

item.title: Diversity of reinsurance, cat bond, ILS, aggregate options a strength for insurers: KBRA
item.description: In the current softening market environment where reinsurance capital is abundantly available, primary carriers are benefiting from a greater diversity of protection options, across traditional reinsurance, insurance-linked securities and a growing availability of aggregate covers, rating agency KBRA has explained. KBRA sees this wider range of risk transfer options as “a strength” for primary insurance carriers. Of course, that point on discipline being necessary goes for the reinsurance, catastrophe bond and broader ILS market’s as well, as the availability of options to deploy capital increases, with carriers buying more.

item.title: Arch Re promotes Soares and Schriber to CEO roles
item.description: According to the announcement, both Soares and Schriber will report to Jerome Halgan, who was recently promoted to CEO of Arch’s Global Reinsurance Group. Arch Reinsurance Group, the reinsurance underwriting arm of global re/insurer Arch Capital Group, has promoted William Soares to Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Arch Reinsurance Ltd. in Bermuda, and Jon Schriber to CEO of Arch Reinsurance Company in the U.S., effective immediately.

item.title: MGA Amulet Specialty launches with multi-line offering
item.description: He is joining Amulet from Talisman Specialty, where he previously served as Underwriting Director responsible for establishing the UK Property and Casualty offering. MISSION-backed specialist managing general agent (MGA) Amulet Specialty has launched with a comprehensive portfolio covering property types, as well as casualty, liability, and business interruption. By blending its specialist underwriting expertise with advanced technology, Amulet aims to eliminate unnecessary administration, enabling the team to focus on building broker relationships and delivering the best client outcomes.

item.title: Guy Carpenter names Sugrue Head of International Business Intelligence
item.description: Guy Carpenter, the reinsurance broking arm of Marsh, has announced the appointment of Dennis Sugrue as Head of International Business Intelligence. In this role, Sugrue will lead the development of the industry risk insights that drive the broker’s thought leadership and client solutions. Sugrue brings over two decades of experience at the intersection of regulation, ratings, capital markets, and re/insurance.

item.title: Favourable commercial insurance market offers opportunities for US buyers: Lockton
item.description: Lockton, a privately owned and independent insurance broker, says conditions remain favourable for US commercial insurance buyers across most classes of cover, creating opportunities to strengthen insurance programmes and secure long-term value. Published quarterly by Lockton, the report provides an overview of current commercial insurance market conditions and the factors shaping insurance programmes. It also examines the increasing influence of artificial intelligence as a widespread and multi-dimensional business risk, outlines approaches that could help insurance buyers make the most of favourable market conditions while strengthening long-term programme performance, and considers the global economic and geopolitical landscape together with its potential influence on insurance market trends.

item.title: AM Best Revises Outlook to Positive for Illinois’ National Group
item.description: AM Best has revised the outlook to positive from stable for the Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings (Long-Term ICR) and affirmed the Financial Strength Rating (FSR) of A (Excellent) and the Long-Term ICR of “a” (Excellent) of OMS National Insurance Company, Risk Retention Group and Fortress Insurance Company (collectively referred to as National Group). The positive outlook of National Group’s Long-Term ICR reflects the continuing improvement in its operating performance, reflecting underwriting metrics that have consistently outperformed its peers. The Credit Ratings (ratings) reflect National Group’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as strongest, as well as its adequate operating performance, neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management.

item.title: Texas Lawmakers Continue Push to Ban TCH Products
item.description: The Texas Senate Committee on Health and Human Services held a public hearing Tuesday to gather information that will shape future legislation on THC products sold in the state. Invited speakers included Allen Police Chief Steve Dye, who became the face of THC raids, and Aubree Adams, the director of Citizens for Safe and Healthy Texas, who actively campaigns against hemp-derived THC products, citing the risks to youth and mental health. Lawmakers made arguments against THC that harkened back to the days of the “Just Say No” campaigns of the ’80s regarding the risks of THC usage and its effects.

item.title: Illinois Governor Signs AI Regulation Bill That Aims to Mitigate Risks
item.description: “Congress and the president ought to be passing similar legislation, but they’ve so far been unwilling, because many are captive to special interests that profit from the industry having no regulation,” Pritzker said before signing the bill. Illinois has chosen our path.” Senate Bill 315 , also known as the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, increases transparency and accountability requirements for the largest artificial intelligence models — those that generate more than $500 million in annual revenue and are trained using massive computing power. The bill mirrors California’s SB-53 and New York’s Responsible AI Safety and Education Act , which were each signed in late 2025.

item.title: Insurance Doesn’t Have an AI Problem...
item.description: Every AI conversation in insurance right now seems to start in the same place. So it might sound strange to say the industry's AI problem isn't a technology problem. I'm the founder and CEO of a design agency that has worked with insurance companies for more than 15 years, and I keep seeing the same pattern.

item.title: QBE launches business risk self-assessment platform in Australia
item.description: QBE Insurance has launched a suite of interactive self-assessment tools designed to help Australian businesses identify and manage operational risks before they lead to losses. Anthony D’Oca, General Manager Commercial & People Risk at QBE Australia Pacific, said the tools are designed to help businesses better understand the factors driving their risk exposure and strengthen resilience through proactive risk management. The assessments are available through RiskAdvance, QBE’s risk management platform, via the Tools & Resources section of the insurer’s Risk Solutions website.

item.title: Brown & Riding launches national private client practice
item.description: Brown & Riding has launched a national Private Client Practice focused on affluent personal lines risks. Everything we do centers on transparency, responsiveness, and creativity so our retail partners feel confident and supported in front of their clients.” – Courtney Kerr, National Private Client Practice Leader. The practice is led by Courtney Kerr and is supported by a team specializing in affluent personal lines, complex personal risk management, and customized insurance solutions for high-net-worth individuals and families.

item.title: Executives on the Move at AXA XL, Chesapeake, AIG, Aon
item.description: Chesapeake Employers’ Insurance Company announced the appointment of John Barnett as senior vice president and chief financial officer, effective June 23, 2026. Barnett brings more than 25 years of financial leadership experience to Chesapeake. AXA XL appointed Matt Rodliff as chief claims officer, Americas, effective July 13.

item.title: Former Dallas Cowboys Defensive End Kneeland Autopsy Reveals Early Stage CTE
item.description: There is also an online chat at 988lifeline.org Former Dallas Cowboys defensive end Marshawn Kneeland, who died by suicide in November 2025 after a high-speed chase with police, had early stage chronic traumatic encephalopathy, a brain abnormality linked to repeated head trauma, his family announced Tuesday. Researchers determined Kneeland, who was 24, was in stage one of four of CTE. The Boston University CTE Center, which investigates the long-term consequences of repetitive brain trauma in athletes and others, analyzed Kneeland’s brain tissue after his death.

item.title: U.S. Forecaster Says El Niño to Strengthen Through Year End
item.description: El Niño will strengthen through the end of the year, with a 97% chance it will persist through early spring 2027, the U.S. A strong to super-strong eastern Pacific-type El Niño event is expected to form this summer and autumn, raising risks for extreme weather such as flooding and heatwaves across China this year and the next, state news agency Xinhua reported, citing forecasts by the National Climate Centre. “There is an 81% chance of a very strong El Niño during October-December that would rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950,” the U.S.

item.title: Aon India hires Taranjeet Walia as senior client director for commercial risk solutions, growth
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA cracks down on illegal promotions and market abuse in first year of new strategy
item.description: The progress we’ve made in the first year demonstrates that a focused and decisive regulator delivers real benefits for consumers and supports growth.' Nikhil Rathi, chief executive of the FCA, said: 'In the past year we've shut down scams, pursued those who abuse markets through the courts, helped hundreds of thousands of consumers access better financial products and cut the cost of regulation for tens of thousands of firms. It also secured a combined 11 years in prison for 2 cases of insider dealing in the first year of its 5-year strategy, according to its Annual report and accounts published today. The FCA led an international crackdown on illegal finfluencer promotions – resulting in 3 arrests and 650 social media takedown requests.

item.title: Europe Posted Its Strongest Venture Funding Quarter In 4 Years As UK Gains, M&A Holds Up
item.description: In Q2, Europe posted its strongest quarter in four years for venture funding, Crunchbase data shows. Europe’s funding deal count subsided last quarter, but mostly at the seed stage. (It’s worth noting, seed stage rounds are often added to the Crunchbase data set after the close of the quarter, so those numbers will increase over time.) The United Kingdom widened its venture-funding lead last quarter, as startups based in the country raised $10.4 billion — not far from the peak in 2021 at $10.8 billion.

item.title: Cat bonds loss-free yield still above average despite three year decline: VP Bank
item.description: While catastrophe bond yields have been on the decline for a number of years now, private bank and asset manager VP Bank AG continues to give the insurance-linked securities asset class a neutral weighting, noting that loss-free yields still remain above average. Commenting on the outlook for cat bonds and ILS through this year’s Atlantic hurricane season, VP Bank highlights that “the occurrence of the El Niño phenomenon offers hope for a year with low losses.” However, the private bank qualifies that statement by noting on tropical storm forecasts, “These figures reflect the number and intensity of storms, but not the risk of them making landfall. VP Bank has been particularly positive on the inclusion of catastrophe bonds and ILS within investor portfolios for their diversification benefits, with this continuing to be the driver for many allocators to the sector.

item.title: PERILS reduces Windstorm Goretti insured market loss estimate to €468m
item.description: PERILS has confirmed that an updated estimate of the insurance market loss from Windstorm Goretti will be published on January 9th, 2027, twelve months after the event end date. Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has updated its estimate of insurance industry losses for extratropical windstorm Goretti, also known as Elli, that affected southwestern England, northern France and Belgium in early January, slightly lowering the total to EUR 468 million. PERILS published its initial industry loss estimate for the windstorm in mid-February, six weeks after the event end date, putting the total at EUR 467 million.

item.title: Marsh remains world’s largest insurance broker with 2025 revenues of almost $27bn
item.description: Marsh, a global insurance brokerage and risk advisory firm, remains the world’s largest insurance broker, with 2025 revenues of almost $27bn, according to data from rating agency AM Best. AM Best’s annual ranking, based on 2025 total revenue, highlights continued growth among the world’s largest insurance brokers. The agency said: “Consistency was the theme in this year’s list as Marsh captured the top spot for the 16th consecutive year.” Marsh increased its revenue by more than $2bn during 2025, allowing the company to maintain its long-standing position at the top of the global broker market.

item.title: Arch Re promotes William Soares and Jon Schriber
item.description: Arch Reinsurance Group, part of Bermuda-based Arch Capital Group Ltd., has appointed William Soares as Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Arch Reinsurance Ltd. Both Soares and Schriber will report to Jerome Halgan, who was named CEO of Arch Global Reinsurance Group last month. in Bermuda, and Jon Schriber as CEO of Arch Reinsurance Company in the U.S., effective immediately.

item.title: MRS-NA announces enhancements to Real Estate Professional Liability solution
item.description: Munich Re Specialty – North America (MRS-NA) has expanded its Real Estate Professional Liability solution, providing specialized protection for real estate professionals facing increasingly complex professional, regulatory, and operational exposures. “Our Real Estate Professional Liability solution is designed to help clients navigate this changing environment with flexible coverage and underwriting expertise tailored to the real estate sector.” The enhanced offering from MRS-NA supports a broad array of real estate specialists, including property managers, leasing agents, appraisers, real estate consultants, asset managers, auctioneers, mortgage professionals, and business brokers. Marcia Blanco, Head of Financial Lines, MRS-NA, said: “As commercial real estate firms continue to expand beyond traditional roles into development, asset management, leasing, consulting, and property operations, professional liability exposures are becoming broader and more challenging to predict.

item.title: Amwins and Dragoneer Consortium Reaffirm $5.3B Bid for Australia’s Steadfast
item.description: Australia’s Steadfast said on Thursday that Amwins Group and Dragoneer Investment had reaffirmed their takeover proposal valuing the insurance broker at A$7.7 billion ($5.34 billion), prompting a four-week extension of the exclusivity period. (Reporting by Kumar Tanishk in Bengaluru; editing by Rashmi Aich) Get the insurance industry's trusted newsletter

item.title: Viewpoint: Insurance, Cybersecurity Are Twin Pillars of Resilience for iGaming Industry
item.description: In this article, Elizabeth Grima of the specialty Lloyd’s broker New Dawn Risk and Craig Lusher of the IT solutions company Continent 8 Technologies argue that resilience, trust and innovation will define the sector’s next phase of growth: with insurance and cyber protection as its twin pillars. Executive summary: Once seen as niche, the iGaming, or the online gambling and sports betting industry, has come of age — now a regulated global market, expanding rapidly and attracting mainstream attention. The iGaming industry has matured into a regulated global business, one of the fastest growing areas of the entertainment industry, home to listed companies and garnering mass mainstream recognition.

item.title: Honda’s Insurance Agency Operations Stall, Services ‘Paused’
item.description: Related: Honda Launches Insurance Agency Powered by VIU by HUB However, a visit to the website today only shows a short message that the services have been “paused,” and insurance products are not being offered or serviced “while we shape a future insurance experience for you.” Existing policyholders are told to contact their insurer or VIU by HUB. Started as a licensed insurance agency for Honda and Acura owners in July 2025, Honda Insurance Services worked with brokerage VIU by HUB to offer quoting and advice for auto, home, renders, condo, pet, motorcycle, boat and recreational vehicle products. About a year after launching, Honda Insurance Solutions is no longer in operation.

item.title: Prudential to Retain 22% Stake in ICICI Prudential Life After Shift from Promoter to Investor
item.description: The post Prudential to Retain 22% Stake in ICICI Prudential Life After Shift from Promoter to Investor appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Prudential Plc has confirmed it will retain its 22% stake in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance, moving from promoter to investor status to comply with Indian regulations tied to its acquisition of Bharti Life Insurance. Prudential Plc has confirmed it will retain its 22% stake in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance, moving from promoter to investor status to comply with Indian regulations tied to its acquisition of Bharti Life Insurance.

item.title: Optalitix Adds Agentic AI to Optalitix Quote, Enabling Natural-Language Access to Underwriting Systems
item.description: The post Optalitix Adds Agentic AI to Optalitix Quote, Enabling Natural-Language Access to Underwriting Systems appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Optalitix has introduced Agentic AI capabilities to its pricing and underwriting platform, Optalitix Quote, letting insurers, reinsurers, and MGAs interact with their underwriting systems using everyday language while retaining existing governance controls. Optalitix has introduced Agentic AI capabilities to its pricing and underwriting platform, Optalitix Quote, letting insurers, reinsurers, and MGAs interact with their underwriting systems using everyday language while retaining existing governance controls.

item.title: KYND Launches Portfolio Analytics to Give Cyber Insurers Portfolio-Wide Exposure Visibility
item.description: The post KYND Launches Portfolio Analytics to Give Cyber Insurers Portfolio-Wide Exposure Visibility appeared first on Insurtech Insights . KYND has launched Portfolio Analytics, a platform designed to help cyber insurers gain visibility into portfolio-wide exposures and track whether insured organisations are addressing emerging cyber risks. KYND has launched Portfolio Analytics, a platform designed to help cyber insurers gain visibility into portfolio-wide exposures and track whether insured organisations are addressing emerging cyber risks.

item.title: Allianz plans layoffs
item.description: Allianz plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as a result of implementing AI solutions. Allianz Partners employs more than 22,000 people. The cuts, which will include early retirements and other options, will amount to 1,500 to 1,800 positions across Europe.

item.title: Starr launches D&O policy suite for UK and US public companies
item.description: Starr Insurance Companies has launched a suite of proprietary commercial directors’ and officers’ (D&O) policy wordings for UK and U.S. According to Starr, the new wordings were developed in response to increasing regulatory scrutiny, shareholder expectations, and governance challenges facing directors and officers of public companies. public companies, expanding its management liability offering for brokers and listed businesses.

item.title: Ember Goes Live with Kyber
item.description: Kyber today announced a partnership with Ember ( embermutuals.com ) to empower its mutual carrier partners with new innovation designed to modernize their claims workflow. Through the partnership, two of Ember Mutual’s carrier partners – CFM Insurance and Forreston Mutual – will gain access to Kyber, enabling faster, clearer, and more consistent communication with policyholders during the claims process. “Kyber gives Ember mutual partners a consistent process while allowing adjusters to focus on the judgment and service that matter most to policyholders.” Kyber is purpose-built for insurers to automate claims letters and forms using no-code templates, compliance-aware workflows, and review controls.

item.title: Premiums Will Skyrocket by 2035, and Discounts Not Enough for Wind Mitigation, Studies Say
item.description: But relying on the incentive of premium discounts is not enough to prompt widespread adoption of mitigation measures, especially for low- and moderate-income households, according to a new study that examined more than 20 years of data from Florida’s Citizens Property Insurance Corp. And property insurers in Florida offer discounts of as much as 44% for some mitigation steps, such as window and door opening protection. Home fortifications, from roof tie-downs to window protections, have been recognized as key factors in reducing losses, claims and premiums in hurricane-prone states like Florida.

item.title: Medical Chronology Software Built for Insurance Defense
item.description: That’s the largest documented in-house claims legal deployment we cite, and it sets the bar for what defense-grade medical chronology software has to do at carrier scale. So when defense paralegals evaluate medical chronology software , the questions look different. Medical chronology software is the tool that takes that week-long manual job and compresses it into a working afternoon, without sacrificing the source-linking you’ll need on cross.

item.title: Vero Insurance New Zealand hires Chloe Donnelly as marine underwriter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: European fund manager Arrow Group to acquire Australian-headquartered W&I MGA Fusion
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh’s Wincy Choi swaps London for Hong Kong for finpro role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Exploring cross-sectoral interconnections in resolution planning
item.description: Understanding the cross-sectoral interconnections and how they play out in a crisis will be central to that review. They say resolution planning is one of the few fields where bank, insurer, and financial market infrastructure experts all agree on something: nobody wants to be the one who actually needs to use the plan. A cross-sectoral perspective is not optional; it needs to be integrated into our thinking.

item.title: ILS portfolios remain focused on resilience despite 2026 hurricane season forecasts: SCOR IP
item.description: In a new report, SCOR Investment Partners has said that while outlooks for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season appear to be more positive compared to recent years, largely due to the emergence of strong El Niño conditions over the summer, portfolio construction remains focused on diversification and resilience, with a key objective of maintaining a high level of shock‑absorption capacity. Beginning with El Niño/La Niña, SCOR Investment Partners explains that current estimates suggest a transition from a weak El Niño in early summer to a strong to very strong event by August–October, coinciding with the peak of the hurricane season. In its report, that focuses on the implications that the Atlantic hurricane season could have for the ILS market, the firm outlined that this year’s season is expected to be “significantly quieter than normal”, with activity projected to well below both recent years and long-term averages.

item.title: Linda Langenberg appointed Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s at Munich Re
item.description: Munich Re, one of the world’s leading reinsurance, primary insurance and risk solutions providers, has appointed Linda Langenberg as Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s. Most recently, she served as Senior Underwriter before being appointed Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s. In her new role, Langenberg will work with Munich Re’s Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s team during a dynamic period for the market.

item.title: Intact sees higher than expected cat and large losses of $416m for Q2’26
item.description: Intact Financial Corporation, a Canadian property and casualty insurer, has reported that catastrophe and large losses were elevated in the second quarter and settled $247 million above the firm’s expectations on a pre-tax basis and net of reinsurance. Intact has confirmed that it also saw an unusually high level of large losses in the second quarter. Intact stated, “These losses included a higher frequency of fire claims and other property losses across various geographies and risk segments.

item.title: Kieran Wilson joins ASR as Senior Energy Underwriter
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks (ASR), the developing-markets-focused re/insurance group, has announced the appointment of Kieran Wilson as Senior Energy Underwriter. Wilson will be reporting to Suzan Pardesi, the Head of Energy and Deputy Active Underwriter for Syndicate 2454, who commented: “Kieran is an experienced underwriter with strong technical energy underwriting expertise and solid broker relationships. In his new position, based in London, Wilson will focus on expanding and underwriting ASR’s energy portfolio, collaborating closely with brokers to develop the group’s oil, gas, and broader energy operations throughout Africa and other emerging markets.

item.title: Deere Settles Lawsuit by FTC, States Over Equipment Repair Restrictions
item.description: The settlement requires Deere to give farmers and independent providers access to the same equipment diagnostic and other repair resources available to authorized dealers for 10 years, and access to new repair resources once more than 50% of authorized dealers have access. According to the FTC, Deere illegally amassed monopoly power over repairs for its farm equipment, leading to service delays and higher prices as well as greater profits. Deere agreed on July 8 to settle a lawsuit by the Federal Trade Commission and five U.S.

item.title: UK Water Regulator Finds Severn Trent Breached Wastewater Duties
item.description: UK water regulator Ofwat found that Severn Trent Plc had “serious failings” in how it deals with wastewater and sewage as part of a broad investigation into practices of utilities in the sector. Ofwat didn’t impose a fine on Severn Trent, which had already identified problems and invested £98 million ($131 million) to address the issues before the regulator opened its case, according to a statement on Wednesday. “Our investigation found serious and unacceptable breaches by Severn Trent Water — that is not in question and the company accepts it,” Lynn Parker, senior director for enforcement at Ofwat, said in the statement.

item.title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strikes
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill on Thursday, after the US struck Iran for a second straight day as a fragile truce between the two sides looked increasingly shaky. The slowdown comes after a spate of Iranian attacks on vessels that had prompted the US strikes , while President Donald Trump also said the ceasefire with Iran was over. Read More: Some War Insurers Advise Shipowners to Pause Hormuz Voyages After Attacks: Sources While liquefied natural gas tanker traffic through the strait remained at a standstill, two empty vessels have recently entered the Gulf of Oman and are heading toward the eastern entrance of Hormuz.

item.title: Why so quiet? Asia (re)insurance market remains committed to hydrogen projects despite relative silence
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Steadfast extends exclusivity period for US$5.4bn Amwins, Dragoneer bid
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zinnia highlights annuity technology partnerships as market demand grows
item.description: Zinnia announced that it is working with life insurers to accelerate annuity product launches and modernize policy administration as carriers invest in technology to meet growing demand for annuities. Bottom Line: While Zinnia highlights its technology partnerships, one employee warns about the overuse of technology. The life and annuity technology company said its platform enables carriers to launch new annuity products in as little as three months, supports straight-through processing rates of more than 95% for new business, and offers same-day policy issuance.

item.title: Arch Insurance launches voluntary employee benefits products
item.description: Arch Insurance North America launched a new suite of voluntary group insurance products for employers, brokers, and agents, expanding its employee benefits offerings with accident medical expense reimbursement, hospital cash indemnity, and annual travel insurance. The new products are designed to supplement employer-sponsored benefits without requiring medical underwriting. The launch expands Arch Insurance’s Accident & Health portfolio, which also includes short-term disability, paid family and medical leave, business travel accident, and Medical GAP insurance.
